events.event_description: Legation established when Lucius Harwood Foote presented credentials as Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary, the first U.S. chief of mission to Korea.

evidence.quote: Lucius Harwood Foote, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1883-02-27. Credentials presented: 1883-05-20. Left post on 1885-02-19.

events.event_description: U.S. Legation closed after Japan assumed direction of Korean foreign relations; Minister Morgan closed the Legation on November 28, 1905.

evidence.quote: Japan assumed direction of Korean foreign relations on 1905-11-17. Note: Morgan closed the Legation on November 28, 1905, and left Korea on December 8, 1905.

events.event_description: Embassy established when John Joseph Muccio presented credentials as Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary, the first U.S. Ambassador to Korea, following the post-WWII restoration of Korean independence.

evidence.quote: John Joseph Muccio, Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1949-04-07. Credentials presented: 1949-04-20. Left Korea 1952-09-08.
